title: Advanced HeroQuest (is Awesome)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7Odyxk-S9f0

Daniel (@dungeondive) makes the case for Advanced HeroQuest as one of the great overlooked dungeon crawlers — and he's not interested in playing it by the book. This deep dive covers the house rules, fan expansions, and component upgrades that transform Games Workshop's 1989 release into something far more compelling than the sum of its yellowing cardboard parts. The Enhanced Advanced HeroQuest fan supplement, the Terror in the Dark official expansion, and a healthy disregard for rules-as-written all feature prominently.

What emerges is a portrait of a game that rewards tinkering — procedural dungeon generation, Skaven lurking behind every door, and a lethality that makes survival feel genuinely earned. Daniel walks through his setup, his preferred rule modifications, and why the random dungeon system still holds up three decades later.

----

What's your favourite game that only really shines once you've house-ruled it into shape?
